business.name: The Elder Pheasant
business.address: 23 Lamb's Conduit Street, Bloomsbury, London WC1N 3NJ

generated.tagline: A Bloomsbury dining room for slow lunches and late dinners
generated.aboutP1: The Elder Pheasant is a small dining room behind a green-tile front, two streets from Russell Square. The kitchen leans on what English cooking does best when it is not trying too hard — short menus, careful sourcing, and a wine list that stays honest about price.
generated.aboutP2: Tables are close enough that you will overhear the table next door, which is part of the point. The lunch crowd is unhurried: writers, locals, the occasional editor. Booking is gentle on Tuesdays. Walk down Lamb's Conduit on a grey afternoon and stay for an early dinner.

signatureDishes.name: Crab on Toast
signatureDishes.line: Brown crab from Cornwall, sourdough, mace butter, parsley — a proper starter

signatureDishes.name: Pheasant Pie
signatureDishes.line: Suet-crust pie, root vegetables, a glass of cider on the side

signatureDishes.name: Sticky Toffee
signatureDishes.line: Warm date pudding, double cream, the version your grandmother promised
